page.title: Marbella Starlite Festival 2026: Yacht Charter Guide & Tips

data.meta_description: Plan your Marbella Starlite Festival 2026 with a yacht charter from Puerto Banús. Compare boats, prices & tips for the ultimate festival experience.
data.summary: Your guide to combining the Marbella Starlite Festival 2026 with a yacht charter from Puerto Banús. Includes boat options, pricing, and insider tips for the best festival experience.
key_facts: Marbella Starlite Festival 2026 runs July–August in the Cantera de Nagüeles.
Charter a boat from Puerto Banús and sail to the festival's coastal venue.
Our fleet includes 20 boats from 6.4m to 29m, all departing Puerto Banús.
Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) from €749 for 2 hours to €2,299 for 8 hours.
Tier B boats (Mangusta 80) from €4,719 for 4 hours, including a jet ski.
All charters include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and VAT.
Book early – festival dates sell out fast for both tickets and boats.
Combine a daytime charter with an evening festival concert for a full day.

faq.q: How far is Puerto Banús from the Starlite Festival venue?
faq.a: Puerto Banús is about 10 km (6 miles) from the Cantera de Nagüeles, where the festival is held. By road, it takes roughly 15–20 minutes. By boat, you can anchor at nearby Cala del Faro or Marbella Marina and take a short taxi ride to the venue.

faq.q: Can I charter a boat for just the festival evening?
faq.a: Yes, you can book a 2-hour charter (€749 for Tier A boats) to cruise before the concert, then return to Puerto Banús and take a taxi to the festival. For a full experience, consider an 8-hour charter (€2,299) to enjoy the coast all day and then head to the festival.

faq.q: Which boats are best for a group going to the Starlite Festival?
faq.a: For groups up to 12 people, the Mangusta 80 (24m) or Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m) are ideal. Both depart from Puerto Banús and offer ample deck space. For smaller groups, the Astondoa 40 (9 pax) or Azimut 39 (11 pax) are cost-effective options.

faq.q: Does the charter price include festival tickets?
faq.a: No, festival tickets are sold separately. We only provide the yacht charter. You can book tickets directly through the Starlite Festival website or authorised vendors. We recommend securing both early, as demand is high.

faq.q: What is included in the charter price?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21% tax). There are no hidden fees – the price you see is the price you pay.

faq.q: Can I anchor near the festival venue to watch from the boat?
faq.a: The Starlite Festival is held inland at the Cantera de Nagüeles, so you cannot see the stage from the water. However, you can anchor at Marbella Marina or Cala del Faro, enjoy a pre-concert cruise, and then take a short taxi ride to the venue.

faq.q: How do I book a yacht for the Starlite Festival 2026?
faq.a: Contact us via WhatsApp for availability and pricing on all boats except Tier A and Tier B. For Tier A (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) and Tier B (Mangusta 80), you can book directly through our website or WhatsApp. We recommend booking at least 2–3 months in advance.

faq.q: What is the best time of day to charter during the festival?
faq.a: Most festival concerts start in the evening (around 8–9 PM). A popular option is a half-day charter (4 hours) from noon to 4 PM, then return to port, freshen up, and head to the festival. Alternatively, a 2-hour sunset cruise (6–8 PM) works well before the concert.

data.body_html:
The Marbella Starlite Festival 2026 returns to the Cantera de Nagüeles, bringing world-class music and entertainment to the Costa del Sol from July to August. Combining a yacht charter with the festival turns a great night into an unforgettable day. Depart from Puerto Banús, cruise the Marbella coastline, and arrive at the festival relaxed and ready. Here’s everything you need to know to plan your Marbella Starlite Festival yacht charter 2026.

Why charter a yacht for the Starlite Festival?

A yacht charter adds a layer of luxury and flexibility to your festival experience. Instead of battling traffic or queuing for taxis, you spend the day on the water, soaking up the Mediterranean sun. After the concert, you return to your boat for a nightcap under the stars. Our fleet departs from Puerto Banús, just 10 km from the festival venue, making it a seamless addition to your evening.

Our fleet for the Starlite Festival 2026

We operate 20 boats from Puerto Banús, ranging from 6.4m speedboats to 29m superyachts. All charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and VAT. Here are the most popular choices for festival-goers:

  • Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) – €749/2h to €2,299/8h. Ideal for small groups wanting a cost-effective, comfortable cruise.
  • Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) – Same pricing as Astondoa 40. Sleek Italian design with ample sunbathing space.
  • Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) – From €4,719 for 4 hours, including a jet ski. Perfect for larger groups who want speed and style.
  • Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) – Pricing on request. A flybridge offers panoramic views and extra social space.
  • Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax) – Pricing on request. The flagship of our fleet, ideal for a VIP experience.

For other boats (Canados 86, Pershing 46, Lagoon 380, etc.), contact us via WhatsApp for pricing and availability.

How to combine a yacht charter with the Starlite Festival

Most festival concerts start around 8–9 PM. Here are three popular itineraries:

  • Full-day charter (8 hours): Depart Puerto Banús at 10 AM. Cruise to Cala del Faro for a swim stop, lunch at a beach club, then return to port. Freshen up and head to the festival by 8 PM. €2,299 for Tier A boats.
  • Half-day charter (4 hours): Depart at 2 PM. Sail to Marbella Marina, enjoy the coastline, then return to port by 6 PM. Taxi to the festival. €1,499 for Tier A boats (approximate – contact us).
  • Sunset cruise (2 hours): Depart at 6 PM. Watch the sunset over La Concha, then dock at 8 PM and head straight to the concert. €749 for Tier A boats.

Where to anchor near the festival

The Starlite Festival is held inland, so you won’t see the stage from the water. However, you can anchor at Marbella Marina (5 minutes by taxi) or Cala del Faro (10 minutes by taxi). Both offer beautiful swimming spots and easy access to the festival. We recommend anchoring at Marbella Marina for convenience – it’s the closest port to the Cantera de Nagüeles.

What to bring and what to wear

The festival dress code is smart-casual. For the boat, pack swimwear, sunscreen (SPF 50+), a hat, and sunglasses. Evenings can be cooler, so bring a light jacket. We provide water, soft drinks, beer, wine, and cava, but you’re welcome to bring your own champagne or snacks. Remember your festival tickets and ID.

Booking tips for the Starlite Festival 2026

Festival tickets go on sale in early 2026, and our yacht charters book up quickly – especially for Tier A and Tier B boats. We recommend booking your boat at least 2–3 months in advance. For the best availability, consider a mid-week charter (Tuesday–Thursday) rather than weekends. Contact us via WhatsApp for real-time availability on all vessels.

Frequently asked questions

How far is Puerto Banús from the Starlite Festival venue?

Puerto Banús is about 10 km (6 miles) from the Cantera de Nagüeles, where the festival is held. By road, it takes roughly 15–20 minutes. By boat, you can anchor at nearby Cala del Faro or Marbella Marina and take a short taxi ride to the venue.

Can I charter a boat for just the festival evening?

Yes, you can book a 2-hour charter (€749 for Tier A boats) to cruise before the concert, then return to Puerto Banús and take a taxi to the festival. For a full experience, consider an 8-hour charter (€2,299) to enjoy the coast all day and then head to the festival.

Which boats are best for a group going to the Starlite Festival?

For groups up to 12 people, the Mangusta 80 (24m) or Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m) are ideal. Both depart from Puerto Banús and offer ample deck space. For smaller groups, the Astondoa 40 (9 pax) or Azimut 39 (11 pax) are cost-effective options.

Does the charter price include festival tickets?

No, festival tickets are sold separately. We only provide the yacht charter. You can book tickets directly through the Starlite Festival website or authorised vendors. We recommend securing both early, as demand is high.

What is included in the charter price?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21% tax). There are no hidden fees – the price you see is the price you pay.

Can I anchor near the festival venue to watch from the boat?

The Starlite Festival is held inland at the Cantera de Nagüeles, so you cannot see the stage from the water. However, you can anchor at Marbella Marina or Cala del Faro, enjoy a pre-concert cruise, and then take a short taxi ride to the venue.

How do I book a yacht for the Starlite Festival 2026?

Contact us via WhatsApp for availability and pricing on all boats except Tier A and Tier B. For Tier A (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) and Tier B (Mangusta 80), you can book directly through our website or WhatsApp. We recommend booking at least 2–3 months in advance.

What is the best time of day to charter during the festival?

Most festival concerts start in the evening (around 8–9 PM). A popular option is a half-day charter (4 hours) from noon to 4 PM, then return to port, freshen up, and head to the festival. Alternatively, a 2-hour sunset cruise (6–8 PM) works well before the concert.

Ready to plan your Marbella Starlite Festival yacht charter 2026? [Browse our full fleet](/ ) and contact us via WhatsApp to secure your preferred boat. We look forward to welcoming you aboard.
